questions.context:
(54-56)
Six people are being interviewed about the garden on the roof of their building.
Jasmine: I loved the idea when Wilber first told-meabout it. We had lots of meetings
with our neighbors, trying tomake them understand why it’s good to build a
garden on the roof. Now people love coming here, and it’s helped many of us
become friends!
ee eoe ee
Wilber: The whole thing wasn’t easy at first. But Jasmine helped a lot. And she
was really good at making people happy to give money for the roof garden.
ceeoe eee
David: My kids love going up there. They sit there watching butterflies and birds.
The roof garden brings them closer to nature.
cee ee ee
Samuel: You want something green? Visit the-park! It’s only one block away! After
the roof garden was built, bugs started flying into my apartment! And the kids
leave mud on the stairs when they come down from the roof!
eee ee Se
Rosie: Our building is now cooler in the-summer; My baby sleeps well even on hot
summer days!
aoee eee
Flora: Guess where these tomatoes are from! Not from the supermarket. They’re
from our roof! Isn’t that wonderful?
questions.stem: What do we know about the six people in the interview?

options.A: They all said good things about the roof garden.
options.B: Some of them were paid to help build the roof garden.
options.C: They all talked about the roof garden before it was built.
options.D: Some of them worked on the plan to build the roof garden.

questions.explanation:
官方答案：C（They all talked about the roof garden before it was built.）
題目重點：先把題幹的限制條件圈出來，再比對最符合條件的選項。
作答步驟：先讀題幹，再回頭對照四個選項，最後檢查是否有單位、對象或關鍵詞被忽略。
題組提醒：這題有前置材料，作答前要先把題組或題幹補充資訊看完。
複習方向：這題來自 103 年會考英語科，可回頭複習同主題的核心觀念與常見判斷方式。

questions.stem: Which of the good things about the roof garden 1s NOT talked about in the interview?

options.A: It brings people in the building closer together.
options.B: It uses the rainwater that falls on the top of the building.
options.C: It makes the building a more comfortable place during summer.
options.D: It gives people in the building a chance to-grow their own food.
